title: Microlicia neogracilis
intro: Microlicia neogracilis, also known as 'Gracilis', is a species of plant in the family Melastomataceae, a family of flowering plants commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Microlicia neogracilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been attributed to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Microlicia neogracilis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Microlicia neogracilis belongs to the family Melastomataceae and is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Microlicia. It is native to Brazil and is part of the order Myrtales in the larger classification of angiosperms. Within the family Melastomataceae, Microlicia neogracilis is one of the many species that are characterized by their unique flower structures. The exact classification of Microlicia neogracilis follows the taxonomy of the plant kingdom: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Myrtales, family Melastomataceae, genus Microlicia, species M. neogracilis. Further classification can be made at the subspecies or infraspecies level, but the most general classification for Microlicia neogracilis places it within the family Melastomataceae.
